about:
Texas public school districts — called Independent School Districts (ISDs) — operate under the Texas Education Agency (TEA) but set their own academic calendars. Most large districts begin the school year in mid-August. Houston ISD, Dallas ISD, Fort Worth ISD, Austin ISD, and Northside ISD (San Antonio) typically open within one to two weeks of each other, though not always on the same date.

Spring Break in Texas falls in mid-March, slightly earlier than East Coast states. The Dallas–Fort Worth Metroplex and Houston metro areas see heavy travel volume during this period, particularly at major airports. Families planning domestic or international travel during Spring Break should book well in advance and expect elevated airfares in the weeks surrounding the break.

Some Texas districts observe additional holidays beyond standard federal observances. Cesar Chavez Day (March 31) appears on some district calendars, particularly in districts with large Hispanic student populations like San Antonio area ISDs. Not all Texas ISDs observe this holiday — check your specific district's official calendar.

San Antonio is home to several major military installations including Fort Sam Houston and Lackland Air Force Base. Families on Permanent Change of Station (PCS) orders to San Antonio most commonly enroll in Northside ISD or North East ISD. Both districts follow standard Texas ISD calendar patterns but families should confirm specific start dates and enrollment requirements directly with the district upon arrival.
planningTips: Texas Spring Break falls in mid-March. DFW Airport and Houston Intercontinental are two of the busiest airports in the country — book flights at least 6–8 weeks in advance to avoid peak pricing.
Houston ISD and Dallas ISD do not always share the same Spring Break week. If your family spans different ISDs, verify each district's dates before booking.

Northside ISD (San Antonio) is one of the fastest-growing ISDs in Texas. Military families arriving at Fort Sam Houston or Lackland AFB should contact the district early — enrollment can take several weeks to complete.
Most Texas ISDs publish the following year's calendar in late winter or early spring. Watch your district's website in January or February to plan the upcoming school year early.

faqs.q: When do Texas schools start in 2026?
faqs.a: Most large Texas ISDs — including Houston ISD, Dallas ISD, Fort Worth ISD, Austin ISD, and Northside ISD — begin the 2026–2027 school year in mid-August. Specific start dates vary by district. Select a district above to see its exact first day of school.

faqs.q: When is Spring Break in Texas for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Spring Break in Texas typically falls in mid-March, though exact dates vary by ISD. Houston and Dallas area districts may not share the same Spring Break week. Check each district's calendar to confirm before booking travel.

faqs.q: What is an ISD in Texas?
faqs.a: ISD stands for Independent School District. Texas public schools are organized as ISDs, each governed by a locally elected school board and operating under the Texas Education Agency (TEA). Major ISDs include Houston ISD, Dallas ISD, Fort Worth ISD, Austin ISD, and Northside ISD.

faqs.q: Do all Texas school districts have the same Spring Break?
faqs.a: No. Spring Break dates are set independently by each Texas ISD. Even neighboring districts in the Dallas–Fort Worth Metroplex or Houston metro area may schedule Spring Break on different weeks. Always verify your specific district's schedule before making plans.

faqs.q: How long is Winter Break in Texas for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Most Texas ISDs observe approximately two weeks of Winter Break from late December into early January. Exact dates vary by district — select a district above to see its specific Winter Break window.

faqs.q: How many instructional days does Texas require?
faqs.a: The Texas Education Agency requires a minimum of 75,600 instructional minutes per school year, equivalent to approximately 180 six-hour school days. Most Texas ISDs schedule exactly 180 student instructional days.

faqs.q: When is the last day of school in Texas for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Most large Texas ISDs end the school year in late May or early June. Select a district above to see its exact last day of school for 2026–2027.

faqs.q: Can I add a Texas school calendar to Google Calendar?
faqs.a: Yes. Each district page on Public School Calendar lets you download an ICS file with all school holidays, breaks, and key dates. Import it into Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or Outlook.
